title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to Analog Electronics;
BJT:
Operating point of BJT- BJT as amplifier (CE configuration)-input chara-
output chara-current amplification factor of BJT;
Power amplifier (using Transistor):
Class A- Class B- Class AB- Class C- (working, chara, power and efficiency
calculation for Class A & Class B, comparison of power amplifiers)
FET:
Construction and characteristics of JFET & MOSFET –Enhancement &
Depletion type-Comparison of BJT & FET
UJT:
Construction-working and characteristics of UJT- Overview of online &
offline UPS- SMPS operation

title: Module 2
content:
Feedback- concept, positive and negative feedback
Oscillator:
Barkhausen criteria-difference between LC & RC oscillator-classification-
working of Hartley and Colpitts Oscillator(using BJT)-analysis of Rc phase
shift oscillator (Using BJT)
555  Timer IC:
Functional diagram -Astable and Monostable Multivibrator using
555(working & derivation of time period)
PLL:
Block diagram- PLL IC 565(Pin diagram)-VCO(Analysis)-Capture range,
lock in range -Applications of PLL

title: Module 3
content:
OP-Amp:
Block diagram of op-amp- Ideal &  Practical OP- Amp parameters-frequency
response- voltage transfer curve-inverting op-amp-non inverting op-amp-
voltage follower-Integrator- Differentiator- comparator-Schmitt trigger-S/H-
V to I & I to V converter, Instrumentation Amplifier
Active Filters using op-Amp:
Difference between active & passive Filters- Active filters: Analysis of First
order HPF & LPF-working of Band pass and Band Stop Filters

title: Module 4
content:
Op- Amp Oscillators and Multivibrators:
Phase shift and wein bridge oscillator-Triangular and sawtooth waveform
generators- Astable and Monostable multivibrators
Data convertors:
DAC: Specifications-Weighted Resistor type, R-2R ladder type
ADC: Specification-Flash type & Successive approximation type
